name: Product designer research-driven — notification center, tests research integration

input.userMessage: Design Notification Center. Research: 58% overwhelmed by notifications, avg 47/day but only 12% acted on. @mentions 34% action rate, likes 2% action rate. Design: (1) default to Actionable tab not All, (2) batch low-signal ('3 people liked your pulse'), (3) 'Mark all as read' prominent. Tabs: Actionable, All, @Mentions. Empty Actionable = celebratory ('You're all caught up!'). Error = calming, not alarming.

expectedBehaviors: Default tab is Actionable (research-driven decision), NOT All
Low-signal notifications are batched ('3 people liked your pulse')
Mark all as read is prominent in the header area
Empty state for Actionable tab is celebratory ('You're all caught up!')
Error state uses calming language, not alarming
Content-guide.md tone differentiation reflected (humor in empty, calm in error)
Stress-test: 500+ notifications, all-read state, network error state
Research data influences design decisions visibly (not just noted, but acted on)
antiPatterns: Default tab showing All notifications (ignoring research directive)
Individual low-signal notifications instead of batched
Mark all as read buried in a menu or de-emphasized
Generic empty state ('No notifications') instead of celebratory
Research data acknowledged but not reflected in design decisions
Using wrong tab names or different tab structure

evaluationCriteria.researchHonored: Is the default Actionable tab, per research directive?
evaluationCriteria.batchingImplemented: Are low-signal notifications actually batched in the design?
evaluationCriteria.markAllReadProminent: Is Mark all as read a primary action, not hidden?
evaluationCriteria.toneConsistency: Does empty state = celebratory and error state = calming?
